content.problemStatement:
You are given arrays AAA and BBB with NNN non-negative integers each.

An array XXX of length NNN is called good, if:


All elements of the array XXX are non-negative;
X1X_1X1​ ∣|∣ X2X_2X2​ ∣|∣ …\ldots… ∣|∣ Xi=AiX_i = A_iXi​=Ai​ for all (1≤i≤N)(1\le i\le N)(1≤i≤N);
XiX_iXi​ &\&& X(i+1)X_{(i+1)}X(i+1)​ &\&& …\ldots… &\&& XN=BiX_N = B_iXN​=Bi​ for all (1≤i≤N)(1\le i\le N)(1≤i≤N).
Find the **maximum** bitwise XOR of all elements over all good arrays XXX.

More formally, find the maximum value of X1⊕X2⊕…XNX_1\oplus X_2\oplus \ldots X_NX1​⊕X2​⊕…XN​, over all good arrays XXX.

It is guaranteed that at least one such array XXX exists.

Note that ∣,&,|, \&,∣,&, and ⊕\oplus⊕ denote the bitwise or, and, and xor operations respectively.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN — the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers describing the elements of the array AAA.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers describing the elements of the array BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** bitwise XOR of all elements over all good arrays XXX.
constraints: It is guaranteed that at least one such array XXX exists.
Sum of NNN over all test cases is less than 3⋅1053\cdot 10^{5}3⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: An optimal good array is X=[0,3,2]X = [0, 3, 2]X=[0,3,2].

For i=1i = 1i=1: A1=X1=0A_1 = X_1 = 0A1​=X1​=0 and B1=X1B_1 = X_1B1​=X1​ &\&& X2X_2X2​ &\&& X3=0X_3 = 0X3​=0.
For i=2i = 2i=2: A2=X1A_2 = X_1A2​=X1​ ∣|∣ X2=3X_2 = 3X2​=3 and B2=X2B_2 = X_2B2​=X2​ &\&& X3=2X_3 = 2X3​=2.
For i=3i = 3i=3: A3=X1A_3 = X_1A3​=X1​ ∣|∣ X2X_2X2​ ∣|∣ X3=3X_3 = 3X3​=3 and B3=X3=2B_3 = X_3 = 2B3​=X3​=2.

The XOR of all elements of XXX is 0⊕3⊕2=10\oplus 3\oplus 2 = 10⊕3⊕2=1. It can be proven that this is the maximum XOR value for any XXX.
Test case 222: An optimal good array is X=[2,1]X = [2, 1]X=[2,1].

For i=1i = 1i=1: A1=X1=2A_1 = X_1 = 2A1​=X1​=2 and B1=X1B_1 = X_1B1​=X1​ &\&& X2=0X_2 = 0X2​=0.
For i=2i = 2i=2: A2=X1A_2 = X_1A2​=X1​ ∣|∣ X2=3X_2 = 3X2​=3 and B2=X2=1B_2 = X_2 = 1B2​=X2​=1.

The XOR of all elements of XXX is 2⊕1=32\oplus 1 = 32⊕1=3. It can be proven that this is the maximum XOR value for any XXX.
